revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:امانوهاشيداته]][[az:Amanoxasidate]][[cs:Amanohašidate]][[de:Amanohashidate]][[en:Amanohashidate]][[eu:Amanohashidate]][[fa:آمانوهاشیداته]][[fr:Amanohashidate]][[he:אמאנוהשידאטה]][[hu:Amanohasidate]][[id:Amanohashidate]][[ja:天橋立]][[ko:아마노하시다테]][[mk:Аманохашидате]][[pl:Ama-no-hashidate]][[ru:Аманохасидатэ]][[sk:Amanohašidate]][[sv:Amanohashidate]][[tr:Amanohashidate]][[uk:Ама-но-хасідате]][[zh:天橋立]] {{short description|Scenery in Japan}} [[File:Amanohashidate view from Mt Moju02s3s4592.jpg|thumb|250px|Amanohashidate view from Mt Monju]] [[File:Amanohashidate, Japan, sun rays and clouds.jpg|thumb|250px|Amanohashidate view from a terrace near Kasamatsu Station]] [[File:Hiroshige A strech of land overgrown with pines.jpg|thumb|250px|Amanohashidate in an [[ukiyo-e]] by [[Hiroshige]]]] [[File:Sesshu - View of Ama-no-Hashidate.jpg|thumb|"View of Ama-no-Hashidate" by {{ill|Sesshū Tōyō|en|Sesshū Tōyō|qid=Q48514|lt=Sesshū Tōyō|1=Sesshū Tōyō}} On the right, [[torii]] of [[Kono Shrine]] is seen. And on extreme right, {{ill|Kanmurijima|en|Kanmurijima|qid=Q6363834|lt=Kanmurijima|1=Kanmurijima}} and [[:en:Kutsujima|Kutsujima]] are also painted.]] '''Amanohashidate''' ({{langx|ja|天橋立|lit=Heaven's bridge}}) is one of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}'s {{ill|Three Views of Japan|en|Three Views of Japan|qid=Q1144867|lt=three scenic views|1=Three Views of Japan}}. The {{ill|shoal|en|shoal|qid=Q28337|lt=sandbar|1=Shoal}} is located in {{ill|Miyazu Bay|en|Miyazu Bay|qid=Q28692458|lt=Miyazu Bay|1=Miyazu Bay}} in northern {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It forms part of the {{ill|Tango-Amanohashidate-Ōeyama Quasi-National Park|en|Tango-Amanohashidate-Ōeyama Quasi-National Park|qid=Q6141097|lt=Tango-Amanohashidate-Ōeyama Quasi-National Park|1=Tango-Amanohashidate-Ōeyama Quasi-National Park}}.
==Location== A thin strip of land connects two opposing sides of Miyazu Bay. This sand bar is {{convert|3.3|km|abbr=on}} long and covered with about 7,000 [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]]s.
It can be viewed from mountains on either side of the bay or it can be traversed on foot. Near the northern end is [[Kono Shrine]], a Shinto Shrine, and the southern end is Chion-ji, a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}}.
On the bar is the Isoshimizu fresh water well cherished since the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, which was selected as one of 100 best springs and rivers in Japan by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qid=Q1125558|lt=Environmental Agency|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}} in 1985.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www2.env.go.jp/water/mizu-site/meisui/data/index.asp?info=55|script-title=ja:環境省選定 名水百選／詳細ページ|author=Ministry of Environment|language=Japanese|accessdate=January 8, 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110612161308/http://www2.env.go.jp/water/mizu-site/meisui/data/index.asp?info=55|archive-date=2011-06-12|url-status=dead}}</ref> [[File:View from the observatory of Nariai-ji Temple in Miyazu, Kyoto 01.jpg|thumb|center|650px|Miyazu Bay and Amanohashidate view from Nariai-ji Temple Observatory]]
==Access== {{ill|Amanohashidate Station|en|Amanohashidate Station|qid=Q4739922|lt=Amanohashidate Station|1=Amanohashidate Station}} on the {{ill|Miyazu Line|en|Miyazu Line|qid=Q6884480|lt=Miyazu Line|1=Miyazu Line}} railway, about two hours from [[:en:Kyoto Station|Kyoto Station]] or [[:en:Osaka Station|Osaka Station]], is located within walking distance from the southern end of Amanohashidate.
==See also== * {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|qid=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|1=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} * {{ill|The 100 Views of Nature in Kansai|en|The 100 Views of Nature in Kansai|qid=Q11656381|lt=The 100 Views of Nature in Kansai|1=The 100 Views of Nature in Kansai}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Wikivoyage}} * [https://www.amanohashidate.jp/lang/en/ Amanohashidate Tourist Association (English page)] {{Commons category-inline}}
